skill.name: meta-search-builder
skill.description: Medical literature search strategy generator. Given a user's natural-language description (e.g., meta-analysis topic, PICOS elements, research question), automatically extract medical entities (disease, intervention, population, outcomes) and generate professional search queries for seven major databases (PubMed, Cochrane, Embase, Web of Science, CNKI, Wanfang, VIP). Useful for developing search strategies for systematic reviews and meta-analyses.
skill.summary: Automatically extracts PICOS elements from research questions and generates ready-to-use search strategies for seven medical literature databases.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 87 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains no executable code — it is purely documentation (SKILL.md, references/databases.md, and a legacy audit JSON file). The backtick execution flags relate to markdown code formatting of database search syntax, not Ruby shell execution. The weak cryptographic algorithm flags arise from the word 'randomized' in medical search context (e.g., 'randomized controlled trial'). The system reconnaissance flags refer to parameter clarification documentation. The hardcoded URL is a legitimate GitHub source attribution. This skill is safe to publish.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: 75 instances of backtick-wrapped text in SKILL.md and references/databases.md were flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard markdown inline code formatting used for database field tags (e.g., TS=, TI=, SU=), MeSH terms, search operators (OR, AND), and example search queries. No Ruby code or shell execution context exists in any file in this skill. All instances are false positives.

low_findings.title: Medical Terminology Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: 10 instances flagged as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are false positives arising from the word 'randomized' in medical research context (e.g., 'randomized controlled trial [pt]', 'randomized [tiab]') and from standard text in JSON metadata labels. No cryptographic operations, algorithms, or key generation exist in any file. This is purely medical literature terminology.

low_findings.title: Parameter Clarification Text Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: 2 instances flagged as 'System reconnaissance'. These are false positives. SKILL.md line 52 documents parameters to clarify before execution ('input path, output path, scope filters'). The audit JSON line 260 contains audit recommendation metadata. Neither constitutes system probing or reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Source Attribution Link Misidentified as Suspicious Network Activity
low_findings.description: 2 instances flagged as 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md line 7. This is a false positive. The URL is a legitimate GitHub source attribution pointing to the skill author's repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). Standard practice for open-source skill documentation.

content.user_title: Generate Medical Literature Search Strategies for Systematic Reviews
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours crafting database-specific search queries for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. This skill automatically extracts PICOS elements from your research question and generates ready-to-use search strategies for seven major medical databases.
actual_capabilities: Extract PICOS elements (Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcome, Study type) from natural-language research questions
Generate PubMed search queries with MeSH terms, Entry Terms, and RCT filters
Create Cochrane Library search strategies using MeSH headings and proper boolean syntax
Build Embase queries with Emtree subject headings, field qualifiers, and synonym expansion
Produce Web of Science queries with field tag prefixes (TS=, TI=, AK=)
Generate search strategies for Chinese databases (CNKI, Wanfang, VIP) with correct field syntax
limitations: Does not execute database searches — only generates query strings for manual use
May miss database-specific advanced features or custom filter options
Cannot access paywalled databases or verify search result accuracy
Chinese database queries use English keywords and may require translation for optimal results

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Search Strategy
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a Cochrane systematic review needs comprehensive search strategies across multiple databases to ensure no relevant studies are missed.

use_cases.title: Rapid Clinical Literature Review
use_cases.description: A clinician needs to quickly find evidence on a treatment's efficacy for a specific condition and requires a targeted search strategy for PubMed.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Literature Search
use_cases.description: A PhD student writing a research grant proposal must demonstrate thorough knowledge of existing literature through structured database searches.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PICOS Search Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to find studies about using metformin for treating polycystic ovary syndrome in adult women. Create a search strategy for PubMed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple single-database query from a basic research question

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Database Systematic Review Search
prompt_templates.prompt: I am conducting a systematic review on the efficacy of cognitive behavioral therapy for treating insomnia in elderly patients. Generate search strategies for PubMed, Cochrane, Embase, and Web of Science.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive multi-database search for a systematic review

prompt_templates.title: Complex Intervention Comparison Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Research question: In patients with type 2 diabetes, how does semaglutide compare to dulaglutide for glycemic control and weight loss? Generate search strategies with PICOS extraction.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparative effectiveness research with specified outcomes

prompt_templates.title: Rare Disease Search Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a search strategy for a systematic review on treatments for pulmonary alveolar proteinosis. Identify all relevant synonyms and generate queries for all seven supported databases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive search for a rare condition requiring broad synonym expansion

output_examples.input: Find studies on bevacizumab for treating ovarian cancer.
output_examples.output:
Extracted PICOS:
- P (Population): ovarian cancer
- I (Intervention): bevacizumab
- C (Comparator): not specified
- O (Outcome): not specified
- S (Study type): RCT

PubMed Search Strategy:
("Ovarian Neoplasms"[MeSH] OR "ovarian cancer"[tiab] OR "ovary cancer"[tiab] OR "ovarian neoplasm"[tiab]) AND ("Bevacizumab"[MeSH] OR "Avastin"[tiab] OR "Mvasi"[tiab]) AND (randomized controlled trial[pt] OR controlled clinical trial[pt] OR randomized[tiab] OR placebo[tiab])

output_examples.input: I need to search for vitamin D supplementation for preventing falls in elderly patients.
output_examples.output:
Extracted PICOS:
- P (Population): elderly patients
- I (Intervention): vitamin D supplementation
- C (Comparator): placebo or standard care
- O (Outcome): fall prevention
- S (Study type): RCT

Cochrane Library Search:
("Vitamin D" OR "Cholecalciferol" OR "Ergocalciferol" OR "25-hydroxyvitamin D") AND ("Accidental Falls" OR "Falls" OR "Fall risk" OR "Fall prevention")

best_practices: Always specify the study type (RCT, cohort, case-control) in your query to ensure appropriate filters are applied to the search strategy.
Review generated search strategies for database-specific syntax before running them in production to catch any formatting issues.
Validate extracted PICOS elements match your intended research question before generating multi-database queries.
anti_patterns: Do not use overly broad keywords without proper MeSH mapping, as this returns too many irrelevant results.
Do not combine terms from different databases without adapting the syntax, as each platform uses unique field tags and operators.
Do not skip the PICOS extraction step, as this leads to incomplete or unfocused search queries.

faq.question: Which databases does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports PubMed, Cochrane Library, Embase, Web of Science, CNKI, Wanfang, and VIP databases.

faq.question: Can this skill run the searches for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates search query strings only. You must copy the queries into the respective database search interfaces.

faq.question: What is PICOS and why is it used?
faq.answer: PICOS stands for Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcome, and Study type. It is a standard framework for structuring clinical research questions.

faq.question: Does this skill support Chinese-language search terms?
faq.answer: The generated queries use English keywords. For Chinese databases like CNKI and Wanfang, you may need to translate terms for optimal results.

faq.question: Can I use this for narrative reviews instead of systematic reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. You can omit the systematic review filters and generate broader search queries for narrative or scoping reviews.

faq.question: How do I specify complex interventions or comparators?
faq.answer: Include the intervention and comparator details in your natural-language description. The skill will extract them as PICOS elements.
